business.headline: Infosys, HCL Tech, TCS fall up to 3%: Why are IT stocks falling today?The Nifty IT index fell 2.67% to 27,525.80, emerging as the worst-performing sector on Dalal Street as of 9:50 am. The decline also weighed heavily on the Sensex, where IT majors were among the biggest losers.

Sensex, Nifty fall amid West Asia tensions; IT stocks tumble over 2%The S&P BSE Sensex fell 263.34 points, or 0.36%, to 73,719.84 in early trade, while the NSE Nifty50 dropped 70.70 points, or 0.30%, to 23,144.25 as of 9:24 am.

Sensex, Nifty opening: Will stock market fall or rise today?Early indicators pointed to a weak start for domestic equities. GIFT Nifty futures were trading at 23,069 at around 7:43 am, indicating that the Nifty 50 could open below Wednesday's closing level of 23,214.95.

OpenDoor lays off entire India team of 250 in AI-driven overhaulOpenDoor is winding down its India operations and moving nearly 250 roles to the United States. The shift to smaller AI-native teams has sparked fresh questions about AI's impact on offshoring.

India scraps excise duty on petrol with 22% to 30% ethanol blendIndia has exempted petrol containing 22% to 30% ethanol from excise duty. The move is aimed at encouraging higher ethanol use and reducing reliance on imported crude oil.

Who borrows the most in India today: Gen Z, millennials or older Indians?India's love affair with credit is growing. As digital lending apps and easy EMIs make borrowing more accessible than ever, one question stands out: who is borrowing the most? Is it young Gen Z, middle-aged millennials, or older Indians?

Sebi proposes new salary disclosure rules for asset managersIn a consultation paper released on Wednesday, Sebi suggested that AMCs should be allowed to disclose the combined remuneration paid to executive-level employees along with the total number of such employees.

Markets end marginally higher as FMCG, banks gains cushion profit bookingThe BSE Sensex rose 64.42 points, or 0.09%, to close at 73,983.18. However, the NSE Nifty50 slipped 27.15 points, or 0.12%, to settle at 23,214.95.

EU widens Russia sanctions net, India-based entities among 50 firms targetedThe European Commission has proposed a new Russia sanctions package that could restrict India-based companies. The move widens scrutiny of third-country trade networks, but it still needs unanimous EU approval.

Zepto moves closer to IPO launch: What investors should watchAccording to the updated filing, Zepto's IPO will consist of a fresh issue of shares worth Rs 8,010 crore and an offer-for-sale (OFS) of 113 million shares by existing investors.

No 8th Pay Commission yet, but a DA hike could be around the cornerFresh inflation data has raised the possibility of a Dearness Allowance (DA) increase from July 2026 for central government employees and pensioners. The likely move could offer interim relief while the 8th Pay Commission continues to examine wider salary and pension demands.

Do you own too many mutual funds? How overdiversification can hurt returnsAdding a new mutual fund every year feels like a smart move. But at what point does diversification turn into overdiversification and start hurting returns? Let's find out.
Do stock market returns really beat FDs after taxes and risk?Ace investor Shankar Sharma has questioned whether stock markets really outperform fixed deposits once taxes and volatility are counted.

Will AI reduce hiring? TCS Chairman explains what lies ahead for IT jobsSpeaking at TCS's 31st annual general meeting, N Chandrasekaran outlined a future where AI agents become an integral part of the workforce.
Equity mutual fund inflows drop 40.4% month-on-month amid global uncertaintyAMFI data showed equity mutual fund inflows fell 40.4% to Rs 22,907.77 crore in May from Rs 38,440.20 crore in April.

Gold falls below Rs 1.5 lakh, silver drops Rs 4,000. Here's whyGold and silver slumped in India after heavy selling in global bullion markets. A stronger dollar, rising oil prices and revived rate hike fears deepened pressure on precious metals.

CleanMax shares jump 15% to record high: Why the stock is rising todayCleanMax and Meta have partnered to develop more than 900 MW of renewable energy capacity in India. The agreement has lifted investor interest as rising data centre and AI demand boosts the outlook for clean power.

Sensex up 500 points: Why are markets rising despite fresh US-Iran escalationSensex rose 500 points, while Nifty also gained. The positive move surprised many investors because geopolitical tensions usually trigger a selloff in risk assets.

Markets open higher as investors shrug off US-Iran conflict; RIL, HUL jump 2%The S&P BSE Sensex rose 337.02 points, or 0.46%, to 74,255.78 in early trade, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 80.35 points, or 0.35%, to trade at 23,322.45 as of 9:27 am.

Meta partners with Reliance for it's first AI data centre in IndiaMeta has partnered with Reliance Industries to lease a 168 MW AI-enabled data centre in Jamnagar. The deal expands their long-running relationship as both companies deepen their AI push in India.

entertainment.headline: Uorfi Javed exposes chilling rape and death threats after The Traitors win Uorfi Javed shared screenshots of trolling, rape threats and death threats after her The Traitors Season 1 win. Her post renewed attention on the abuse women routinely face on social media.

Why Bharathiraja returned his Padma Shri in 2009: Shall we break it or throw it? As tributes poured in after filmmaker Bharathiraja's death, attention returned to his 2009 protest over the Sri Lankan civil war, where he returned his Padma Shri award. The episode captured how his public activism stood alongside his long influence on Tamil cinema.

Taylor Swift's courtside appearance sparks buzz over who was seated behind her Taylor Swift's appearance at NBA Finals Game 4 came with an unexpected twist as she found herself just rows away from a familiar figure from her past. Here's what happened.

Daniel Radcliffe, Tom Felton spark Harry Potter nostalgia at Tony Awards after-party Daniel Radcliffe and Tom Felton reunited at a Tony Awards after-party at The Carlyle in New York, serving pure Harry Potter nostalgia.

David Harbour says false Millie Bobby Brown reports triggered mental breakdown David Harbour has said false reports about Millie Bobby Brown triggered a mental health breakdown last year. He said their disagreements were part of a family-like bond and that they still work together.

Obsession breaks horror box-office records, crosses $200 million globally Curry Barker's horror film Obsession has crossed $200 million worldwide to become Focus Features' highest-grossing release. Its run, aided by record holds and strong word of mouth, has also gathered momentum in India.

Peddi box office Day 7: Ram Charan's film slows down, nears Rs 400 crore globally Ram Charan's Peddi posed an estimated Rs 7.55 crore nett in India on Day 7. Despite the weekday dip, the film is nearing the Rs 400 crore-mark.

Peddi: How Janhvi Kapoor ended up stealing Ram Charan's thunder Peddi was always meant to be Ram Charan's shot at pan-India stardom. But the focus since release has been on Janhvi Kapoor for reasons far from the obvious.

Pawan Kalyan is sold on Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar for this reason Pawan Kalyan has praised Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar for its bold storytelling. The Andhra Pradesh Deputy Chief Minister also opened up about his views on cinema across languages and revealed why another recent film left a lasting impact on him.

Rs 370 ki biryani and the cost of a woman's consent A viral comedy clip showed a Gurugram man joking about expecting an ROI (return on investment) after spending Rs 370 on a date. The backlash widened into a debate on consent, misogyny and the laughter that helped spread it.

Inside Selena Gomez's emotional Instagram tribute to husband Benny Blanco Selena Gomez surprised fans with 16 never-before-seen photos of Benny Blanco. The touching Instagram post comes during a period of separation and reveals more about their relationship.

Jeremy Strong takes over as Zuckerberg in The Social Reckoning teaser The first teaser for The Social Reckoning is out, offering a glimpse of Jeremy Strong as Mark Zuckerberg. The follow-up to The Social Network revisits Facebook's influence and a story that changed the internet.

Pakistani fan travels 20 km to meet Imtiaz Ali, AR Rahman at border, shares note A Pakistani fan travelled 20 km to the Attari-Wagah Border to meet Imtiaz Ali and AR Rahman during promotions for Main Vaapas Aaunga. He later shared a heartfelt note on social media, recalling the emotional cross-border encounter.
Bharathiraja to be cremated with full state honours in his hometown on June 11 Filmmaker Bharathiraja will be cremated with full state honours at his farmhouse in Theni district on June 11.

NBK111 glimpse: Nandamuri Balakrishna unveils new avatar on 65th birthday The makers of NBK111 released a special glimpse titled Entry of an Era on Nandamuri Balakrishna's 65th birthday, offering fans their first look at the actor's new avatar.

Kichcha Sudeep's nephew Sanchith on nepotism: My uncle can't act on my behalf Mango Pachcha actor Sanchith Sanjeev opened up about carrying Kichcha Sudeep's legacy, navigating nepotism, and why success ultimately depends on winning over audiences.

Is Alia's Alpha a copy of French thriller La Femme Nikita? Fans spot similarity Alpha's teaser has triggered online comparisons with cult films La Femme Nikita and American Sniper. The reaction has added a plagiarism debate to the buzz around YRF's female-led spy thriller.

Ahead of Welcome 3, Raveena Tandon shares throwback Mohra pic with Akshay, Suniel Raveena Tandon shared a series of BTS pictures from Welcome To The Jungle, but it was a throwback photo from the sets of Mohra featuring Akshay Kumar and Suniel Shetty that stole the spotlight.

Khushboo Patani slams misogynistic comments amid Pranit More row: Educated fools Khushboo Patani criticised comedian Pranit More after an audience member's 370 biryani remark about expecting a return drew outrage. The backlash widened as both men apologised and the audience member's employer ended his employment.

general news.headline: TMC's Prakash Baraik resigns as Rajya Sabha MP, third such exit within a weekWith Prakash Chik Baraik's resignation, the party's strength in the Rajya Sabha is set to fall to 10 MPs. Sources further indicated that as many as three more TMC Rajya Sabha MPs could resign within the next week, potentially deepening the crisis for the Mamata Banerjee-led party.

India scraps excise duty on petrol with 22% to 30% ethanol blendIndia has exempted petrol containing 22% to 30% ethanol from excise duty. The move is aimed at encouraging higher ethanol use and reducing reliance on imported crude oil.

US strikes Iran overnight, Tehran retaliates as Gulf spirals into deeper conflict  Live UpdatesAs Washington and Tehran exchanged fresh blows, the fallout was felt across multiple countries in the region. The developments have heightened fears of a wider regional conflict involving US forces and their allies stationed across the Middle East.

OpenDoor lays off entire India team of 250 in AI-driven overhaulOpenDoor is winding down its India operations and moving nearly 250 roles to the United States. The shift to smaller AI-native teams has sparked fresh questions about AI's impact on offshoring.

Why does POK have a separate president, PM and flag? It's a Pak conspiracyIf Pakistan-occupied Kashmir (POK) is controlled by Pakistan, why does it have a separate president, PM and flag? This is Pakistan's clever tactic, to fake autonomy while it keeps oppressing and killing Kashmiris in POK. Explained in details here.

IND A vs AFG A Live: Prabhsimran hits fifty after Sooryavanshi out for 44India A (IND) vs Afghanistan A (AFG) Live Score, Tri-Series 2026: India A (128/2 in 15 ov). Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, who had dominated the Afghanistan A bowlers with nine boundaries, was dismissed for 44 off 22 balls by Abdullah Ahmadzai. Ruturaj Gaikwad has joined Prabhsimran Singh, who has hit a fifty in just 39 balls.

Woman doctor under fire for comment on male corpses' private parts at Pranit More showDuring an interaction at Pranit More's show, Sejal Pawar spoke about joking and comparing the sizes of male cadavers' private parts. She has issued an apology on Instagram.

Not jobs, not dowry: Why men in this UP village are still unmarriedDespite government claims of piped water reaching every household in Mudhara village, residents have alleged that not a single drop has flowed from taps installed under a multi-crore drinking water scheme.

Elon Musk, population collapse in India and that nonsense called end of humanityElon Musk and other population collapse theorists exaggerate declining birth rates to stoke fears over demographic change and mask xenophobic anxieties about race, identity and immigration rather than any genuine threat to humanity's survival.

Zomato rejects 'Rs 370 biryani bhejdu' notification claim amid Pranit More rowZomato said a viral screenshot claiming to show a consent-related app notification was fake. The clarification has fed into the wider debate around the Pranit More show's Rs 370 biryani controversy and consent.

10 rounds in 5 seconds: Bishnoi gang shoots gym owner dead, warns of more attacksA gym operator was shot dead by motorcycle-borne attackers near Fawwara Chowk in Hansi during an early morning exercise session. A young woman was injured by bullet fragments as police and forensic teams began an investigation after CCTV footage surfaced.

Bill Gates says Epstein used affair details to blackmail him after he cut tiesBill Gates appeared before the House Oversight and Government Reform Committee as lawmakers continued their investigation into the handling of the Epstein case and the network of influential people who interacted with the disgraced financier.

IIT Kanpur hires Nisarga, the teen hacker who exposed flaws in CBSE's OSM portalAfter identifying vulnerabilities in CBSE's OSM portal, 19-year-old cybersecurity researcher Nisarga Adhikary joins IIT Kanpur's C3iHub as an OSINT and Threat Intelligence Engineer, reflecting the growing recognition of responsible security research in India.

Woman doctor found dead in Jalandhar, husband accused of affair, loans in her nameA government doctor was found dead at her Jalandhar home, and police registered a case on her father's complaint. Her family has alleged abuse, unauthorised loans and an affair, while investigators await post-mortem and forensic findings.

FIFA World Cup arrives broken. Can football fix it again?The wait is over. Football's grandest stage is set once again. Yet before the spotlight turns to Lionel Messi, Cristiano Ronaldo and a new generation eager to make its mark, the 2026 FIFA World Cup arrives under a cloud of scrutiny, carrying more off-field questions than any tournament would want on the eve of kick-off.

E20 today, E30 tomorrow? Should you rethink your next petrol vehicle purchase?While E20 is now the norm, the notification of E22, E25, E27 and E30 standards raises fresh questions about how future fuel policies could impact today's petrol vehicles and in the future diesel will likely be blended with 15 per cent isobutanol.

Indian-origin entrepreneurs create unicorn record in US amid immigration backlashWhile Indian immigrants find themselves at the centre of the US's anti-immigrant push, a new report shows that India-born entrepreneurs founded or co-founded more US unicorns than immigrants from any other country. Indians topped the list with 96 unicorns, ahead of founders from Israel, the UK, China, Canada and Russia.

Congress moves Supreme Court over Meenakshi Natarajan's RS nomination rejectionCongress has approached the Supreme Court after Meenakshi Natarajan's Rajya Sabha nomination from Madhya Pradesh was rejected. The dispute over a Telangana proceeding has sharpened the contest for the third seat ahead of the June 18 poll.

Why 8 in 10 Delhi-NCR patients rush to the internet right after visiting a doctorA survey has found that many Delhi-NCR patients search online after doctor consultations because many feel that their questions remain unanswered.

Why young Indians are risking fines and arrest to explore abandoned placesYou can now easily find Indian Surbex (urban exploration) content creators across social media platforms. The dramatic footage of deserted malls, crumbling factories and unfinished buildings, long-forgotten adventure parks often attract millions of views.

Indian-origin man defrauds US bank of $100 million using fake documents, arrestedUS authorities arrested California-based businessman Mahender Makhijani over an alleged nearly USD 100 million bank fraud. Prosecutors said fake title records made loan collateral appear stronger and exposed the lender to losses.

Monsoon has stalled: What's blocking its vigorous march across India?After racing across large parts of southern and northeastern India, the southwest monsoon has entered a slower phase, raising questions about what comes next.

No subscription needed: Here's how you can watch World Cup for free in IndiaThe FIFA World Cup 2026 is all set to begin, with the fans in India eagerly waiting to watch each game. Here's how you can watch the matches for free in India.

Harmanpreet Kaur vows fearless cricket as India prepare for Pakistan clash India captain Harmanpreet Kaur has vowed to play fearless cricket as her side gears up for a blockbuster start to its ICC Women's T20 World Cup 2026 campaign against arch-rivals Pakistan at Edgbaston in Birmingham on Sunday.

LA 2028 working to avoid repeat of World Cup visa issues in US: IOC chief Los Angeles Olympics organisers are working closely with U.S. authorities to ensure smooth entry procedures and avoid the visa complications that affected several stakeholders at the FIFA World Cup, IOC president Kirsty Coventry said on Wednesday.

FIFA World Cup 2026 by the numbers: Breaking down the largest edition ever The 2026 FIFA World Cup will open a 48-team tournament across the United States, Canada and Mexico on June 11. Its scale is unmatched, with 104 matches, 1,248 players and several records under threat.

Ben Stokes made a mistake, but shouldn't be sacked as Test captain: Michael Vaughan Michael Vaughan has defended Ben Stokes amid scrutiny over his curfew breach, arguing that while the England captain made a mistake, the incident does not justify removing him from the Test captaincy.

Making sure right people come to US: Trump on FIFA World Cup visa row US President Donald Trump said his administration is working to ensure that "the right people" are allowed into the country for the FIFA World Cup 2026 as the United States prepares to host the tournament.

Hitman in range: Rohit Sharma gets into groove for India vs Afghanistan ODIs Rohit Sharma returned to India's white-ball nets and gradually found his range after a lay-off. The session eased immediate worries and showed a senior batter measuring himself against his own standards.

Explosive Richa Ghosh finds form, but India lose final T20 World Cup warm-upWomen's T20 World Cup 2026: Richa Ghosh hit 68 off 36, but India lost to England in Cardiff in their final warm-up match. Her counterattack eased concerns before Sunday's World Cup opener, though India's top order still remains under scrutiny.

FIFA World Cup coverage in India headlined by Igor Stimac, Gurpreet SandhuWith the FIFA World Cup 2026 just days away, ZEE5 has assembled a star-studded multilingual commentary team featuring Bhaichung Bhutia, Gurpreet Singh Sandhu, Igor Stimac and I.M. Vijayan to bring the tournament closer to football fans across India.

After missing IPL, MS Dhoni promotes inaugural edition of Jharkhand T20 leagueMS Dhoni attended the opening ceremony of the inaugural Jharkhand T20 League in Ranchi on Wednesday. His presence gave the new tournament a high-profile start.

FIFA World Cup fever grips Kerala as God's own country prepares for tournamentWorld Cup fever has engulfed Kerala, with flags, cutouts and flex battles stretching across the state. Beneath the carnival sits a familiar truth: deep football culture, thin grassroots support and one more emotional wait for Messi, Ronaldo and Neymar.

Ben Stokes, Gus Atkinson dropped from England Test squad after nightclub incidentEngland captain Ben Stokes and Gus Atkinson have been dropped for the second Test against New Zealand after breaching a midnight curfew at a London nightclub. Stokes is reportedly considering his future as the Test captain of England.

Security breach sees Messi, Argentina's World Cup squad's passport details leakedAn official team sheet at Alabama's Jordan-Hare Stadium reportedly exposed the passport details of Lionel Messi and Argentina's entire squad before the Iceland friendly. The leak raised fresh concerns over player privacy and data security days before Argentina begin their World Cup defence.

Manchester United captain Fernandes, Keane bury hatchet after candid chatRoy Keane said he has spoken to Bruno Fernandes after a row over comments about the Premier League assist record between the duo. The exchange closed a misunderstanding that Fernandes said had distorted his original remarks.

Haiti will wear Poland's flag on their FIFA World Cup 2026 jersey. Here's whyHaiti are set to make their second appearance at the FIFA World Cup this year, but there seems to be a small tribute on the kit to Poland with the presence of their flag.

World Cup, Group J preview: Defenders Argentina prepare for Messi's farewellFIFA World Cup 2026: Lionel Messi's Argentina prepare for one last dance for the legendary footballer. Argentina have been drawn against Algeria, Austria and Jordan in the Group stage.

Australian Badminton: Sindhu storms into 2nd round, Tanvi upsets 5th seedIndia's PV Sindhu and emerging star Tanvi Sharma marched into the second round of the Australian Badminton Open. Sindhu, having returned to Top 10 in singles rankings hammered Peru's Ines Castillo in straight games.

Priyank Panchal launches India's first athlete-led sports education hubSportsversity has launched an athlete-first sports education platform with Priyank Panchal as strategic adviser. The venture aims to open structured pathways into non-playing careers across India's expanding sports industry.

Shubman Gill becomes top-ranked Indian Test batter, Jasprit Bumrah retains top spotIndia captain Shubman Gill is now the country's top Test batter in the latest ICC rankings, while Jasprit Bumrah remains the No.1 Test bowler. Harry Brook climbs to the global summit.

Pakistan cricket to decide futures of captain Masood, Sarfaraz over weekendPakistan Cricket Board officials will meet this weekend to decide whether Shan Masood and Sarfaraz stay in charge after the 0-2 defeat in Bangladesh. The review could trigger a wider reset, with Salman Ali Agha, Younis Khan and governance changes all in play.

Breakout Test spinner Manav Suthar signs County Championship contractIndia's latest Test sensation, Manav Suthar has bagged a stint in County Cricket. The off-spinner is set to play for Warwickshire for two matches this season.

Somalia gives hero's welcome to FIFA World Cup referee denied entry by US: VideoSomalia gave Omar Abdulkadir Artan a hero's welcome after US authorities denied him entry for the 2026 FIFA World Cup. The award-winning referee landed in the capital city of Mogadishu on Wednesday, June 10.

Curacao to Cape Verde: The unmissable minnows at FIFA World Cup 2026From Curacao's historic debut to long-awaited returns for Haiti and DR Congo, alongside Cape Verde's tactical brilliance, the expanded 48-team FIFA World Cup gives football's ultimate minnows a shot at global immortality. Here is all that you need to know about them.

Camera comes crashing down in football match, crew narrowly escapes huge mishapWhile the FIFA World Cup has been muddled with on and off-field controversies, things are not better for the federations outside of the global tournament as well. The latest disaster has taken place in a friendly between Hungary and Kazakhstan, where a spider-cam suspended with wires above the stadium, came crashing down on the pitch.

Asian Games: No Babar, Shaheen as Sahibzada Farhan to lead Pakistan's young squadPakistan have handed Sahibzada Farhan the captaincy of a youthful squad for the 2026 Asian Games, leaving out several established white-ball stars. The selection signals a shift in focus following recent tournament disappointments.

England can dare to dream but are not heavy favourites, says Thomas TuchelEngland manager Thomas Tuchel believes his side have the quality to challenge for the World Cup title, but insists they cannot be considered favourites given their long wait for success on football's biggest stage.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
